name: property-features

fields.name: property_id
fields.comment: FK to PostgreSQL properties.id — used to filter vector search to candidates from hard filter

fields.name: room_type
fields.comment: Bedroom, Bathroom, Kitchen, Living Room, Dining Room, Garage — used for room_context filtering

fields.name: instance_index
fields.comment: Which instance of this room type (0, 1, 2...)

fields.name: feature_text
fields.comment: All features as natural text: 'accent wall, modern fireplace, walk-in closet, hardwood floors'. Searchable for full-text keyword matching.

fields.name: feature_vector
fields.comment: Embedding of feature_text — used for semantic/synonym matching (e.g., 'covered pool' matches 'enclosed pool')

fields.name: city
fields.comment: Denormalized for optional vector search filtering

_comment_data_flow: 1. Properties are stored in PostgreSQL (hard filters + geo queries)
2. Each room instance is indexed here as a separate document
3. Search pipeline: PostgreSQL narrows candidates → property_ids sent here as filter
4. Azure AI Search does vector similarity on feature_text within filtered set
5. room_type filter enforces 'bedrooms with X' stays in Bedroom documents only

bedrooms_with_fireplace.comment: property_ids come from PostgreSQL hard filter results

covered_pool_synonym.comment: Vector search handles synonym: 'covered pool' matches 'enclosed pool' or 'indoor pool'
